mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust labor market, which translates into higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive equity returns. This is a persistent, always-on signal of economic health.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a rolling 3-year period, indicating no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, an acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) is interpreted by markets not as a tightening risk, but as a positive signal that the economy is escaping a deflationary trap, boosting risk appetite and equity valuations.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between 3-month changes in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An interaction where industrial production (supply) growth outpaces retail sales (demand) growth signals an unintended inventory build-up. This divergence points to future margin compression and earnings disappointments, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month % change in INDPRO > 3-month % change in RSAFS
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recessionary recovery, falling unemployment is unambiguously positive, signaling healing in the labor market without immediately triggering fears of monetary tightening. This improvement in the economic outlook drives equity prices higher.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is 6.6, well above pre-crisis lows
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 5.0%, instances of month-over-month declines are not followed by statistically significant positive 20-day returns compared to a baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production is a direct signal of weakening manufacturing and business investment. This leading indicator of economic slowdown negatively impacts expected corporate profitability and leads to lower equity market returns.
failure_condition: The median 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is not negative in the quarters following a negative 3-month change in INDPRO.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, unexpected drop in housing starts (HOUST) signals a rapid deterioration in consumer confidence and residential investment, which has a strong negative multiplier effect on the broader economy, causing a rapid repricing of equities over a short horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST monthly value drops > 10% from previous month
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following a trigger event, the S&P 500 shows a positive mean return, contradicting the hypothesized negative shock.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflationary environment, identified by accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with decelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1), is highly toxic for equities. This combination compresses corporate margins and forces contractionary monetary policy, leading to sharply negative returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year CPIAUCSL change is positive while year-over-year GDPC1 change is negative.
failure_condition: Over a walk-forward test, periods meeting the trigger condition do not exhibit statistically significant negative 60-day returns compared to all other periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) concurrent with a low policy rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that monetary policy is ineffective at stimulating the labor market. This signals a persistent economic slump and poor earnings outlook, causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 for 60 days when the trigger condition is met fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 10-year backtest.
